results.title: pointsSum.slope Not Updated After Nominee Removal and Votes Revocation
results.reasoning: Yes. The audit explicitly reports that removeNominee fails to subtract the nominee’s slope from pointsSum[nextTime].slope, leaving the global sum’s slope inflated and corrupting subsequent _getSum() decay and relative-weight calculations (Items 1 and 33). This is the same root cause, code path (VoteWeighting.removeNominee and its effect on pointsSum), and mechanism described in [H-01]. The suggested remediation (subtract the removed nominee’s slope from pointsSum at removal and/or handle via revoke) also aligns, so fixing as per the report would fix the described issue.

results.title: Arbitrary tokens and data can be bridged to GnosisTargetDispenserL2 to manipulate staking incentives
results.reasoning: The described issue is an access-control/authentication flaw in GnosisTargetDispenserL2.onTokenBridged: the callback accepts arbitrary tokens and payloads without verifying the L1 sender, allowing forged staking instructions to be processed via _receiveMessage. The audit content, while covering several L2 dispenser issues (e.g., redeem re-verification, DoS, gas limit handling on GnosisDepositProcessorL1), does not mention onTokenBridged, lack of origin verification, or arbitrary payload acceptance on GnosisTargetDispenserL2. None of the listed findings would be fixed by removing/guarding onTokenBridged, so the specific vulnerability was not detected.
